snapshot.scope: Independent review of the bounded architecture-reset timebox: Day-10 proof, Day-14 expiry to normal P0-P9 priority, AR3 foreground stop, authoritative-source AR1 proof, minimal efficacy evidence ownership, and unchanged status.

reviewers.independence: Product Planning reviews only contracts and executable governance; Development still owns every implementation deletion, rewrite, security test, package and runtime artifact.

dimensions.summary: The Playbook keeps AR1-AR3 as the only foreground reset sequence and returns later architecture work to the existing P0-P9 queue.
Playbook, decision, manifest and verifier agree on Day 10 proof, Day 14 expiry, historical AR4 and status-neutral evidence ownership.
Existing Requirement, Exit and Phase claims remain unchanged; the optional AR1 proof reports current debt without inventing completion.
The executable authoritative-source proof and expiry rule prevent architecture work from becoming an indefinite global feature pause.
One existing manifest owns timebox, proof and body-free sample fields; no effectiveness document or new trace tier is added.

verification.evidence: Staged architecture contract, timebox fields, authoritative-source exclusions and AR1 proof mutation guard pass.
Existing Requirement, Exit and Phase projections remain unchanged after the timebox policy bind.
The full Planning governance suite passes after semantic-lock and reviewed-snapshot regeneration.

coordination.compatibility: AR1-AR3 implementation contracts remain intact; only foreground duration and priority after expiry change, with AR4 retained as historical inventory.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning owns proof and metric definitions; Project Management collects existing body-free Git, CI and task evidence without Requirement, Exit or Phase promotion.

nonBlockingRisks: The raw human grep has one known negative-truth locale hit; only the executable authority-source Node proof owns AR1 completion.
The ten-PR sample is an initial body-free baseline, not a Phase 1 dashboard or a product-status claim.
